title: headers.rs: Fixed 256-byte buffer panics on large tail payloads
description:
File: rust/lichen-schc/src/headers.rs
Lines: 407, 571, 716, 841

Category: CORRECTNESS

The build functions (build_coap_udp, Icmpv6EchoProfile::build, RplDioProfile::build, RplDaoProfile::build) use fixed 256-byte stack buffers (coap_buf, icmp_buf) but do not validate that the tail payload fits before copying.

Example at line 407:
```rust
let mut coap_buf = [0u8; 256];
...
coap_buf[COAP_FIXED_HEADER..COAP_FIXED_HEADER + tail.len()].copy_from_slice(tail);
```

If tail.len() > 252 (for CoAP) or > 248 (for ICMPv6), this will panic with index out of bounds.

Impact: Any CoAP payload larger than 252 bytes causes a panic during packet reconstruction.

Fix: Add bounds check before copy, or use a dynamically-sized buffer, or return an error if tail exceeds limit.
